description: Create a measurement region. Measurement regions are disks in 2D and spheres in 3D. The measurement calculation is triggered whenever a clean command is invoked, or whenever a FISH intrinsic that queries a measured value is invoked. Measurement regions can calculate porosity, coordination number, stress, strain, and particle size distributions.
notes: Measurement regions are disks (2D) or spheres (3D)
Measurements calculated when 'model clean' is called or when FISH intrinsic queries measurement
Can measure: porosity, coordination number, stress, strain, size distribution
Use 'bins' keyword to enable particle size distribution calculation
Tolerance controls accuracy vs. speed trade-off for porosity calculation
For clumps, voxelization approach used to calculate intersection volumes
Position can be specified as vector or individual x/y/z components

methods.description: PFC Python API provides measure module for creating and managing measurements
methods.note: Command interface may be more convenient for simple setups

notes: Python SDK has measure-related methods in itasca.measure module
Exact method signatures may vary - consult PFC Python documentation
Command interface provides simpler syntax for basic measurement creation
Python interface offers more programmatic control and querying

command_vs_python.python:
# Python SDK approach - consult PFC documentation for exact syntax
# itasca.measure module provides measurement functionality

workflow: 1. Create measurement: measure create radius 5.0 tolerance 0.001
3. Query results: measure list or FISH intrinsics
common_use_cases.notes: High tolerance (0.001) gives accurate porosity for quality control

workflow: 1. Create with bins: measure create bins 100 0.0 2.0 radius 10.0
3. Export data: measure dump command or history
4. Plot cumulative volume percentage vs. diameter
common_use_cases.notes: Bins parameter enables size distribution calculation

workflow: 1. Create region 1: measure create id 1 position (0,0,0) radius 3.0
2. Create region 2: measure create id 2 position (10,0,0) radius 3.0
4. Compare: measure list shows both regions
common_use_cases.notes: Multiple regions allow spatial property mapping

workflow: 5. Track porosity, stress, etc. evolution over time
common_use_cases.notes: Use with history to monitor measurement changes

measured_quantities.description: What can be measured within the measurement region

porosity.tolerance_effect: Lower tolerance = more accurate but slower calculation

coordination_number.description: Average number of contacts per body
coordination_number.calculation: Total contacts / number of bodies in region
coordination_number.use: Characterize packing density and contact network

stress.use: Analyze stress state in specific regions

triggers.use_case: Manual control over when measurements are updated

triggers.description: Querying a measured value automatically recalculates if needed

measurement_triggering.note: Measurements not calculated continuously - only when triggered
measurement_triggering.recommendation: Call model clean before querying measurements to ensure current values

bodies_intersecting_boundary.challenge: Complex intersection calculation, especially for clumps
bodies_intersecting_boundary.approach: Process in order of decreasing volume
bodies_intersecting_boundary.stopping_criterion: Stop when remaining volume < tolerance * total volume

tolerance_values.0.001: High accuracy (0.1% tolerance) - slower but precise
tolerance_values.0.05: Low accuracy (5% tolerance) - faster but less precise

tolerance_explanation.recommendation: Use default (0.01) unless high precision required or performance critical

bins_explanation.purpose: Discretize size distribution into bins for analysis
bins_explanation.bin_count: Number of bins (e.g., 100 for fine resolution)
bins_explanation.body_classification: Each body binned by volume-equivalent sphere diameter
bins_explanation.output: Cumulative volume percentage for each bin
bins_explanation.export: Use measure dump to export to table for plotting
bins_explanation.example: bins 100 0.0 0.5 creates 100 bins from diameter 0.0 to 0.5

position_specification.description: Two ways to specify measurement center position

vector_form.use_when: Convenient for copying from other sources

position_specification.note: Both forms produce identical results - choose based on convenience

clump_intersection_calculation.description: How clump-measurement intersection volumes are calculated
clump_intersection_calculation.challenge: Clumps are complex shapes - exact intersection is computationally expensive
algorithm: 1. Create bounding box around clump pebbles
3. Test each box: inside clump? inside measurement? intersecting?
4. Sum volumes of boxes fully inside both
5. Apply convergence correction for unresolved intersections
clump_intersection_calculation.accuracy: Efficient and accurate without full geometric calculation
clump_intersection_calculation.note: Low tolerance values don't necessarily improve accuracy beyond algorithm limits

warnings.message: Measurements are NOT calculated automatically during cycling - must call model clean
warnings.mitigation: Explicitly call model clean before querying measurements

warnings.message: For tolerance > 0, porosity will generally be slightly overestimated
warnings.mitigation: Use lower tolerance (e.g., 0.001) for high-precision requirements

warnings.message: Particle size distribution requires bins keyword - otherwise not calculated
warnings.mitigation: Always specify bins if you need size distribution data

warnings.message: Measurement regions are disks in 2D, spheres in 3D - position-z only valid in 3D

troubleshooting.issue: measure create succeeds but no measurements available
solutions: Measurements not calculated yet - call model clean to trigger
Use measure list to verify measurement was created
Query measurements after model clean
Check if measurement region contains any bodies
Verify bins keyword was specified in measure create
Call model clean to trigger calculation
Check if measurement region contains bodies
Use measure dump to export size distribution data
Specified ID already exists - use different ID
Delete existing measurement: measure delete id <i>
Omit id keyword to auto-assign next available ID
Use measure list to see existing IDs
Increase tolerance: tolerance 0.05 (faster but less accurate)
Default tolerance 0.01 is usually good balance
Very low tolerance (< 0.001) can be slow for complex clumps
Consider if high precision is really needed
Check position - is measurement region in correct location?
Increase radius to encompass more bodies
Use measure list to verify position and radius
Visualize measurement region in GUI if available

performance_considerations.creation_cost: Very fast - just defines measurement parameters
performance_considerations.calculation_cost: Varies with region size, body count, and tolerance

tolerance_impact.high_tolerance: Faster calculation, slightly less accurate porosity

performance_considerations.clump_overhead: Clumps more expensive than balls due to voxelization
performance_considerations.recommendation: Use default tolerance (0.01) unless specific requirements

id_management.auto_assignment: If id keyword omitted, next available ID assigned
id_management.manual_assignment: Use id keyword to specify exact ID
id_management.uniqueness: Each measurement must have unique ID
id_management.recommendation: Let PFC auto-assign unless you need specific IDs for organization

keywords.name: bins
keywords.description: Specify the number of bins ( i ) and the lowest and highest ( fl and fh ) bin edges for the size distribution calculation. All bodies (e.g., clumps and balls) that fall within the measurement region are binned based on the {area in 2D; volume in 3D} equivalent {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D} diameters. The cumulative volume percentage (between 0 and 1) of bodies is calculated and can be exported to a table with the measure dump command. Table entries hold the average values of each pair of bin boundaries and the corresponding computed percentages.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Measurement ID. If not specified, the next available ID is assigned.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Position of the center of the measurement object. Default to the origin of the global axis system.

keywords.name: radius
keywords.description: Radius of the measurement object. Default is 1.0

keywords.name: tolerance
keywords.description: Specify the relative tolerance for accumulating the volume of bodies that intersect the measurement region in the porosity calculation. The volumes of bodies that fall completely within the measurement region are summed as \(V_s\) ; \(V_{\cap}\) holds the sum of body volumes that intersect the measurement region and a list, intersectList , of these bodies is created. The bodies in intersectList are revisited in order of greatest volume. When visiting a body in intersectList , the volume of the body is removed from \(V_{\cap}\) and the volume of intersection is added to \(V_s\) . When \(V_{\cap} / (V - V_s) \leq f\) , then no more intersecting bodies are visited. This guarantees that the actual porosity will be within a relative tolerance of f from the calculated value. For f , not 0, the porosity will generally be slightly overestimated. By default, f = 0.01. For clumps, a voxelization approach is used to calculate the volume of intersection. The {square in 2D; cubical in 3D} axis-aligned bounding box enclosing all pebbles is recursively bisected into boxes five times. At each level, each box is tested for inclusion in both the clump and the measurement region. Retained for further refinement are boxes that are inside the measurement region and intersect the clump surface, those that are inside the clump and intersect the measurement region surface, and those that intersect both the measurement region surface and clump surface. The volumes of the boxes that fall completely within the clump and measurement region are added to the intersection sum. A correction is applied based on the convergence of the volume with each level of refinement (using a relationship for the logarithm of the length of a side of a box at the current refinement level and the accumulated volume to that level) to estimate the intersection volume in an efficient and accurate way. Thus low percentage values will not translate into more accurate estimations of the volume of intersection, as such calculations can be very time consuming.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: \(x\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement object. Default is 0.0

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: \(y\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement object. Default is 0.0

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: \(z\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement object. Default is 0.0

examples.description: Create measurement region with radius 4.0 at origin, high accuracy porosity (0.1% tolerance)
examples.use_case: Measure porosity, coordination number, stress, and strain with high precision

examples.description: Create measurement with ID 1, 100 bins from 0.0 to 0.5 diameter, radius 4.0
examples.use_case: Generate particle size distribution curve for analysis

examples.description: Create measurement region centered at (5.0, 3.0, 2.0) with radius 2.5
examples.use_case: Measure properties at specific location in model

examples.command: measure create position-x 10.0 position-y 5.0 position-z 3.0 radius 3.0
examples.description: Create measurement region using individual coordinate specifications
examples.use_case: Alternative syntax for specifying measurement position

examples.command: measure create id 5 radius 1.0 bins 50 0.1 1.0 tolerance 0.005
examples.description: Comprehensive measurement setup with all major parameters
examples.use_case: Complete measurement configuration for detailed analysis

examples.command:
measure create radius 5.0
model clean
measure list
examples.description: Create measurement, trigger calculation, then list results

The cumulative volume percentage (between 0 and 1) of bodies is calculated and can be exported to a table with the measure dump command.
